summary.en: Danish toy company (founded 1932 by Ole Kirk Christiansen) whose plastic-brick system was for decades a textbook hits-driven physical-product business. After near-bankruptcy in 2003-2004, LEGO opened up to user-generated content in two distinct long-tail moves: LEGO Factory (2005-2014) let users design custom sets in software and shipped the exact bricks; LEGO Ideas (CUUSOO 2008 → renamed Lego Ideas 2014) lets fans submit set designs that the community votes into commercial production with revenue share to the original creator. Strategyzer's flagship physical-product Long Tail example. The case contrasts the traditional toy industry archetype with both LEGO long-tail moves so the pattern's range becomes visible — from one-user-one-product custom POD to many-buyers-one-curated-fan-design.
summary.zh: 丹麦玩具公司,1932 年由 Ole Kirk Christiansen 创立。其塑料积木系统在数十年里一直是教科书式的「爆款驱动 + 实体产品」模式。2003-2004 年濒临破产后,乐高用两种不同形态的长尾动作向用户生成内容开放:LEGO Factory(2005-2014)让用户在软件中自由拼搭并按订单配齐零件发货;LEGO Ideas(CUUSOO 2008 → 2014 改名)则让粉丝提交套装设计,由社区投票,得票最高的进入正式生产,并和原作者分成。Strategyzer《商业模式新生代》中长尾模式的旗舰「实体产品」示例。本案例把传统玩具行业基线与乐高的两次长尾动作并排对照 —— 从「一个用户 → 一套定制」的实体 POD,到「一个粉丝设计 → 多买家爆款」的社区策展,完整呈现长尾模式在物理产品场景下的取舍。
